content.text:
<user_query>
@/Users/parth/Parth/Dev/Projects/loan-analyser I wanna export this to PDF, however when I tried to export it, I was getting an blank page. Please fix this issue.
Also, whatever I give to chatbot, it gives wrong answer. It lakes in the calculation. Ex, on 33 lakh loans with 7.2% interest rate, I asked how much month reduce if I pay 3 lakh, and it gives me 13 month, however the answer was around 38 month. Please identify an issue, make an improvement to get an accurate answer. You can use another modal as well, which is good at mathematical calculation.
Please verify above things 2-3 times and give me working code.
</user_query>

I’ll fix both issues end-to-end: first I’m checking the PDF export pipeline to remove the blank-page bug, then I’ll inspect the chat calculation logic/model setup and harden it for accurate prepayment month-reduction math.

**Planning initial React-doctor use**

I’ve loaded the React QA workflow; now I’m reading the exact PDF and chat files to pinpoint the root causes before patching.

**Inspecting chat route types**
**Identifying potential message state bug and exploring currency utils**

I found two concrete root causes: PDF capture is racing/rendering off-canvas, and chat relies mostly on LLM guesses instead of deterministic amortization math. I’m now patching types + chat payload so the API can run exact calculations before asking the model.

**Designing deterministic loan logic**

I’m now rewriting the chat API to do deterministic amortization calculations for prepayment/tenure questions (so it can’t hallucinate month savings), and then only use the model for natural-language phrasing.

**Designing comprehensive route rewrite**
**Planning type checks and PDF export fixes**

Next I’m applying a PDF-render hardening patch in the report component itself (stable root id + disabling chart animation during export), which is the other common cause of blank captures.

**Adjusting PDF report styles**

I’ve finished the code changes for both paths; now I’m running lint/build checks and then I’ll run calculation spot-checks (including your 33L/7.2%/3L example) multiple times.

**Preparing lint and type checks**

I hit a sandbox restriction while running numeric spot-check scripts, so I’m rerunning the verification outside the sandbox to finish the 2–3 round accuracy check you asked for.

**Planning script rerun with permissions**

Build/type-check passes. I’m doing one final React-specific diagnostic scan (`react-doctor`) before I hand over, so you get a cleaner confidence check on these UI/data-flow changes.

**Running react-doctor check**

Implemented. I fixed both issues in code.

## What I changed

- **PDF blank export fix**
  - Updated `src/hooks/useExportPdf.ts`:
    - waits for fonts + 2 animation frames + render stabilization before capture
    - checks the report element is actually renderable (`width/height/scrollHeight > 0`)
    - sets safer `html2canvas` options (`backgroundColor`, `windowWidth`, `windowHeight`)
    - uses `onclone` to reposition the cloned report root for reliable capture
  - Updated `src/components/pdf/PdfReport.tsx`:
    - added stable root id: `pdf-export-root`
    - disabled Recharts animations (`isAnimationActive={false}`) for PDF charts to avoid capture race/blank frames

- **Chatbot math accuracy fix**
  - Updated `src/app/api/chat/route.ts`:
    - added deterministic calculation path for loan questions (esp. **“if I prepay X, how many months reduce?”**), using your real amortization engine (`calculateAmortization`) instead of pure LLM guesswork
    - parses Indian amount formats (`3 lakh`, `₹3,00,000`, `2 cr`, etc.)
    - computes exact scenario from current loan + current prepayments + rate changes
    - defaults hypothetical prepayment date to next EMI month if date not specified
    - upgraded model default to `gpt-4.1-mini` with fallback to `gpt-4o-mini` for non-deterministic questions
    - made route work even when `OPENAI_API_KEY` is absent for deterministic queries
  - Updated `src/hooks/useChat.ts` + `src/types/chat.ts`:
    - sends richer context to backend (`emiDebitDay`, baseline without-prepayment metrics, prepayment/rate-change arrays)

## Verification (ran multiple times)

- `npm run lint` ✅
- `npm run build` ✅ (ran twice after final changes)
- Deterministic math check (3 scenarios) ✅
  - prepay `2L` -> ~`25` months reduced
  - prepay `3L` -> ~`37` months reduced
  - prepay `5L` -> ~`57` months reduced
- End-to-end route check for your style of query ✅
  - “if I prepay 3 lakh how many months reduce” returned deterministic tenure reduction + interest saved
- Direct amortization engine check also showed expected scale (3L prepay saves large tenure, in the ~high-30s / low-40s depending EMI/date assumptions)

Your `38` vs `13` complaint is exactly addressed: it no longer guesses that number from LLM text generation.

## Files touched

- `src/hooks/useExportPdf.ts`
- `src/components/pdf/PdfReport.tsx`
- `src/app/api/chat/route.ts`
- `src/hooks/useChat.ts`
- `src/types/chat.ts`

If you want, I can add one more improvement: force chatbot to always include the exact assumed prepayment date in response (so month-difference confusion is fully transparent).

**Summarizing multi-point verification and fixes**
